pattern: Removing one element only disturbs two neighboring pairs, so precomputed adjacent prefix lengths plus prefix and suffix maxima answer every removal in constant time.
hints: Recomputing all adjacent prefixes from scratch for every removal repeats almost the same work each time.
Deleting index i destroys the pairs (i-1, i) and (i, i+1) and creates the single new pair (i-1, i+1); every other adjacent pair is untouched.

Precompute the prefix-length of each original adjacent pair, build prefix-max and suffix-max arrays over them, and for each i combine the max of the untouched pairs with the prefix length of words[i-1] and words[i+1].

edge_inputs.description: single word leaves no adjacent pair
two words, removing either leaves nothing
no two adjacent words share any prefix
the best pair is the one bridged after a removal
removing the middle word bridges two equal neighbors
prefix length capped by the shorter word

stress.description: random short words over a tiny alphabet so many adjacent prefixes are nonzero
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 600))
    words = []
    for _ in range(n):
        L = rng.randint(1, 5)
        words.append(''.join(rng.choice('ab') for _ in range(L)))
    return [words]
